title: Unveiling Science: Lab Reports & The Scientific Method
description: This vocabulary pack introduces essential terms for understanding lab reports and the scientific method, tailored for 6th-8th grade English Language Learners.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A flow chart showing steps: Observe, Question, Hypothesize, Experiment, Analyze, Conclude.
A student looking closely at a plant with a magnifying glass.
A thought bubble with a question mark and a lightbulb.
A scientist writing 'If... then...' on a whiteboard.
A person in a lab coat mixing chemicals in beakers, with test tubes around.
Two arrows pointing in different directions, indicating change or difference.
A hand adjusting a dial or lever, showing something being changed intentionally.
A measuring tape or a thermometer showing a result, indicating something being measured.
Two identical plants, one with a 'treated' label and one with a 'normal' label, showing the normal one.
A spreadsheet or bar graph with numbers and labels.
A person looking at a chart or graph with a magnifying glass, thinking.
A scientist writing 'The hypothesis was supported/not supported' on a notebook.
An open notebook with sections like 'Introduction', 'Methods', 'Results', 'Conclusion'.
Numbered steps or a checklist with arrows indicating sequence.
A collection of common lab equipment: beaker, test tube, Bunsen burner, goggles.
A clear bar graph or line graph showing outcomes of an experiment.
Two students talking and looking at a lab report, exchanging ideas.
A person wearing safety goggles and gloves in a lab, with a 'safety first' sign.

worksheets.instructions_en: Match the English vocabulary word with its Spanish translation.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Empareja la palabra del vocabulario en inglés con su traducción al español.

items.content_l2: Método Científico

worksheets.instructions_en:
Complete each sentence with the correct scientific vocabulary word from the word bank.
Word Bank: Scientific Method, Hypothesis, Experiment, Independent Variable, Dependent Variable, Data, Analysis, Conclusion, Lab Report, Procedure
worksheets.instructions_l2:
Completa cada oración con la palabra de vocabulario científico correcta del banco de palabras.
Banco de palabras: Método Científico, Hipótesis, Experimento, Variable Independiente, Variable Dependiente, Datos, Análisis, Conclusión, Informe de Laboratorio, Procedimiento

items.content: The ______ is a step-by-step process scientists use to investigate questions.
items.content_l2: El ______ es un proceso paso a paso que los científicos usan para investigar preguntas.

items.content: Before starting the experiment, we wrote a ______ predicting what would happen.
items.content_l2: Antes de comenzar el experimento, escribimos una ______ prediciendo lo que sucedería.

items.content: The thing we changed in the experiment is called the ______.
items.content_l2: Lo que cambiamos en el experimento se llama ______.

items.content: The thing we measured is called the ______.
items.content_l2: Lo que medimos se llama ______.

items.content: We recorded all our ______ in a table during the experiment.
items.content_l2: Registramos todos nuestros ______ en una tabla durante el experimento.

items.content: The ______ section of the lab report explains the steps we followed.
items.content_l2: La sección de ______ del informe de laboratorio explica los pasos que seguimos.

items.content: After collecting data, we performed an ______ to find patterns.
items.content_l2: Después de recopilar datos, realizamos un ______ para encontrar patrones.

items.content: Our ______ stated whether the hypothesis was supported or not.
items.content_l2: Nuestra ______ indicó si la hipótesis fue apoyada o no.

worksheets.instructions_en: Read each sentence. Use context clues to figure out which vocabulary word fits best. Write the word on the line.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Lee cada oración. Usa las pistas del contexto para determinar qué palabra de vocabulario encaja mejor. Escribe la palabra en la línea.

items.content: Maria put on her goggles and gloves before entering the lab. She always follows ______ rules.
items.content_l2: María se puso sus gafas y guantes antes de entrar al laboratorio. Ella siempre sigue las reglas de ______.

items.content: The students wrote down everything they saw happening in the beaker. They were making careful ______.
items.content_l2: Los estudiantes anotaron todo lo que vieron suceder en el vaso de precipitados. Estaban haciendo ______ cuidadosas.

items.content: After the experiment, the teacher asked the class to talk about what they learned and share ideas. This part is called the ______.
items.content_l2: Después del experimento, la maestra pidió a la clase que hablaran sobre lo que aprendieron y compartieran ideas. Esta parte se llama la ______.

items.content: The beakers, test tubes, and thermometers were all listed under the ______ section of the lab report.
items.content_l2: Los vasos de precipitados, tubos de ensayo y termómetros estaban todos listados en la sección de ______ del informe de laboratorio.

mini_book.title: The Scientific Journey: Understanding Lab Reports

pages.text_en: Title: The Scientific Journey: Understanding Lab Reports
pages.text_l2: Título: El Viaje Científico: Comprendiendo los Informes de Laboratorio

pages.text_en: What is the Scientific Method? It's a way scientists explore the world. It has steps to help answer questions and solve problems.
pages.text_l2: ¿Qué es el Método Científico? Es una forma en que los científicos exploran el mundo. Tiene pasos para ayudar a responder preguntas y resolver problemas.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: What is the Scientific Method? It's a way scientists explore the world.

pages.text_en: Step 1: Observation & Question. First, you notice something (Observation). Then, you ask a question about it (Question).
pages.text_l2: Paso 1: Observación y Pregunta. Primero, notas algo (Observación). Luego, haces una pregunta al respecto (Pregunta).
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Step 1: Observation & Question. First, you notice something (Observation).

pages.text_en: Step 2: Hypothesis & Experiment. You make an educated guess (Hypothesis). Then, you design a test (Experiment) to see if your guess is right.
pages.text_l2: Paso 2: Hipótesis y Experimento. Haces una suposición informada (Hipótesis). Luego, diseñas una prueba (Experimento) para ver si tu suposición es correcta.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Step 2: Hypothesis & Experiment. You make an educated guess (Hypothesis).

pages.text_en: Step 3: Variables & Control Group. In an experiment, you change one thing (Independent Variable) and measure another (Dependent Variable). A Control Group helps you compare.
pages.text_l2: Paso 3: Variables y Grupo de Control. En un experimento, cambias una cosa (Variable Independiente) y mides otra (Variable Dependiente). Un Grupo de Control te ayuda a comparar.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Step 3: Variables & Control Group. In an experiment, you change one thing

pages.text_en: Step 4: Data & Analysis. You collect information (Data) during your experiment. Then, you look closely at the Data to find patterns (Analysis).
pages.text_l2: Paso 4: Datos y Análisis. Recopilas información (Datos) durante tu experimento. Luego, examinas de cerca los Datos para encontrar patrones (Análisis).
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Step 4: Data & Analysis. You collect information (Data) during your experiment.

pages.text_en: Step 5: Conclusion. Finally, you write what you learned. Did your experiment support your Hypothesis? This is your Conclusion.
pages.text_l2: Paso 5: Conclusión. Finalmente, escribes lo que aprendiste. ¿Tu experimento apoyó tu Hipótesis? Esta es tu Conclusión.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Step 5: Conclusion. Finally, you write what you learned.

pages.text_en: What is a Lab Report? A Lab Report is a written summary of your entire experiment. It tells others about your Question, Hypothesis, Procedure, Data, and Conclusion.
pages.text_l2: ¿Qué es un Informe de Laboratorio? Un Informe de Laboratorio es un resumen escrito de todo tu experimento. Les cuenta a otros sobre tu Pregunta, Hipótesis, Procedimiento, Datos y Conclusión.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: What is a Lab Report? A Lab Report is a written summary of your entire experiment.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and define key vocabulary related to the scientific method and lab reports in English and Spanish, enhancing their comprehension of scientific inquiry.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary using flashcards or the mini-book. Use the tracing worksheet for spelling practice and the matching worksheet to reinforce English-Spanish connections. The coloring worksheet can be used as a fun review or for early finishers. Encourage students to create their own simple lab report using the learned terms.
materials_needed: Science notebooks or paper for additional writing
differentiation_tips: **For struggling learners:** Pair students with stronger English speakers. Provide sentence starters for discussions. Allow them to draw instead of write where appropriate.
**For advanced learners:** Challenge them to write short sentences or paragraphs using the vocabulary words. Ask them to design a simple experiment and outline its steps using the new terms.
**For all learners:** Incorporate real-life examples of the scientific method (e.g., 'Why does my plant need sun?'). Use visuals consistently.
